title: Authorization Token
description: The Authorization Token is produced by Stage 5 (Authorization) of the Governed Execution Protocol. It records the authorization decision made by a human approver or delegated policy authority. It is consumed by Stage 6 (Execution Gate), which validates the token's signature, expiration, single-use status, and intent binding before releasing execution. The authorization_id is referenced in receipts and ledger entries.

authorization_id.description: Unique identifier for this authorization decision (UUID v4)

intent_id.description: Reference to the canonical intent this authorization applies to

decision.description: Authorization decision: ALLOW permits execution, DENY blocks it, ESCALATE routes to a higher authority

approver_id.description: Verified identity of the authorizing entity (must differ from the actor_id in the canonical intent per INV-03)

approval_timestamp.description: UTC Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the authorization decision was made

expiration_timestamp.description: UTC Unix timestamp in milliseconds after which this authorization is no longer valid

single_use.description: When true, the token is consumed upon first use and cannot authorize a second execution

signature.description: ECDSA-secp256k1 signature over the canonical JSON representation of this token (excluding the signature field itself)
